name: Taichi (GPU Parallel Programming)

performance.notes: Compiles Python to efficient GPU/CPU code; comparable to hand-written CUDA

agent_readiness.notes: Python-embedded domain-specific language for high-performance parallel programming. Compiles to CUDA, Metal, OpenGL, Vulkan, CPU. @ti.kernel decorator. Data-oriented programming with taichi Fields. Physics simulation, particle systems, differentiable simulation. Automatic differentiation. GGUI for real-time visualization. Wider backend support than Triton (not just NVIDIA). Used for physics-based simulation, computer graphics, scientific visualization. MIT founder Yuanming Hu.

gotchas: Taichi-specific programming model (not standard Python semantics)
Not designed for deep learning training (use PyTorch/JAX)
Company pivoted to AIGC — library development slower recently
best_when: Physics simulation (fluid, cloth, rigid body) in Python
Scientific computing needing GPU without CUDA vendor lock-in
summary: Taichi is a Python-embedded DSL for high-performance parallel programming. Compiles to CUDA, Metal, OpenGL, Vulkan, and CPU. @ti.kernel decorator, data-oriented Fields, automatic differentiation. Cross-platform GPU support (unlike Triton's CUDA-only). Best for physics simulation, particle systems, and scientific visualization in Python needing portable GPU acceleration.
